source_document: Connecticut SB 1103 (An Act Concerning Artificial Intelligence)
passage_text: A deployer of a high-risk artificial intelligence system shall complete an impact assessment for any high-risk artificial intelligence system deployed by the deployer on or before the later of (A) February 1, 2026, or (B) ninety days after the date on which the high-risk artificial intelligence system is first deployed by the deployer. Such impact assessment shall include, but not be limited to: (1) A statement of the purpose of the high-risk artificial intelligence system and its intended benefits; (2) An analysis of whether the high-risk artificial intelligence system poses any known or foreseeable risk of algorithmic discrimination; (3) A description of the categories of data processed as inputs and the outputs generated; (4) An overview of the safeguards implemented to address the risks identified, including any human oversight measures.

obligation.subject: A deployer of a high-risk artificial intelligence system
obligation.action: complete an impact assessment including statement of purpose, algorithmic discrimination risk analysis, data input/output categories, and safeguard descriptions

timeline.timeline_text: on or before the later of (A) February 1, 2026, or (B) ninety days after the date on which the high-risk artificial intelligence system is first deployed

threshold_exception.threshold_condition: ninety days after the date on which the high-risk artificial intelligence system is first deployed by the deployer, or February 1, 2026, whichever is later

evidence_spans.text: on or before the later of (A) February 1, 2026, or (B) ninety days after the date on which the high-risk artificial intelligence system is first deployed

ambiguity.ambiguous_text: known or foreseeable risk of algorithmic discrimination
ambiguity.interpretation_notes: The standard for what constitutes a 'foreseeable' risk of algorithmic discrimination is subjective. Different deployers may have different levels of technical sophistication and thus different abilities to foresee discrimination risks.
ambiguity.suggested_clarification: Define specific testing methodologies or statistical thresholds that establish whether a risk of algorithmic discrimination is 'foreseeable'
